Explanation: The report presents a technically competent analysis of chromatographic data with clear methodology and well-structured sections. Strengths include detailed methodology describing AsLS baseline correction, peak detection algorithms, and waste zone identification, along with identification of meaningful trends (Q column peak heterogeneity, waste zone elimination in later runs). Writing quality is generally good with appropriate technical terminology. However, the report has notable limitations: it relies entirely on code appendices for results (no summary tables or figures in main text), making visual presentation weak; the analysis is preliminary (Step 1 only), limiting depth; and originality is modest as the approach applies standard signal processing techniques. The appendices contain extensive code but lack actual output figures referenced in section A.1. Discussion adequately interprets findings but acknowledges multiple unresolved questions requiring future work.
